title: Trade Desk stock climbs after company nabs Nasdaq-100 index inclusion
text: Shares of Trade Desk Inc. TTD, +2.20% were rising 4% in Thursday's premarket trading after Nasdaq Inc. title: The Trade Desk: Remarkable Surge That I Didn't Anticipate
text: I turned cautious on The Trade Desk stock too early this year and should have let its recovery prove its worth. The company's ability to deliver and guide above 20% growth in the first half was remarkable, demonstrating its market leadership, even as its peers pulled back. TTD is expensive, but its valuation remains well below its 2020 highs. Also, it's expected to continue gaining leverage as it gains share against its industry.

title: The Trade Desk: Profitability Shines, Price Raises Eyebrows
text: The Trade Desk, Inc. faces a challenge due to its expensive valuation, which raises concerns about the investment thesis. Despite its superb management, buying the stock at a high valuation contradicts the principle of investing with a margin of safety. The market's indiscriminate expansion of multiples indicates a lack of discernment, potentially impacting the stock's performance.

title: Brokers Suggest Investing in The Trade Desk (TTD): Read This Before Placing a Bet
text: Investors often turn to recommendations made by Wall Street analysts before making a Buy, Sell, or Hold decision about a stock. While media reports about rating changes by these brokerage-firm employed (or sell-side) analysts often affect a stock's price, do they really matter?

title: 1 Green Flag for The Trade Desk in 2023, and 1 Red Flag
text: The Trade Desk stock is expensive, and that can turn any good business into a bad investment. To grow at an eye-popping rate, the company only needs to take a small sliver of business from Alphabet -- and things appear to be pointing in that direction.
